observations: Pass rates are identical overall (98% each), but the failures differ: with_skill failed meta-description for eval 1, without_skill failed it for eval 3.
With_skill uses ~48% more tokens (38.8k vs 26.2k avg) and takes ~68% longer (235s vs 140s avg), due to reading skill references and running the quality checklist.

The key qualitative difference is flower diversity: with_skill deliberately chose different flowers for Uberlândia (Gerberas, Orquídeas, Margaridas, Astromélias) to avoid duplicating existing entries, while without_skill used the standard set (Lírios, Rosas, Crisântemos, Gerberas).
With_skill mentioned cremation 7 times vs 4 for without_skill in the crematory eval, showing better type-specific adaptation.
With_skill was more cautious with unverified information (omitted specific address for Crematório when uncertain), while without_skill included a specific address.
The meta-description boundary assertion is the only failure across all runs - both with and without skill struggle with this, suggesting the instruction could be reinforced.
